sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven are in poor form with no wins in their last 3 league matches, conceding heavily (10 goals). KRC Genk also struggles, with no wins in their last 3 league games, but they show more attacking intent with consistent scoring.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, results are split with each team winning once. Genk won the most recent match 2-0, while Leuven secured a 3-1 victory at home previously.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Both teams have struggled defensively, with Leuven conceding the most goals in the league (10) and Genk failing to keep clean sheets. Genk's superior possession and shot creation could give them a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Leuven's home form shows vulnerability, often conceding late goals. Genk's ability to create big chances and their historical edge as the first to score in head-to-heads may influence the outcome.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, KRC Genk holds a 2-1 lead over Oud-Heverlee Leuven at The King Power AT Den Dreef Stadium. Genk struck early with two goals from Zakaria El Ouahdi, showcasing their clinical finishing in the first half. Leuven responded late through Roggerio Nyakossi’s header, pulling one back and giving themselves a lifeline going into the second half. The game has been intense, with both teams creating opportunities, though Genk has largely controlled possession and looked more threatening overall.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: KRC Genk has dominated the ball and shown greater composure in the final third, converting their chances efficiently. Their expected goals reflect a higher likelihood of scoring compared to Leuven, and their superior duel-winning ability has helped them maintain control. Oud-Heverlee Leuven, on the other hand, has struggled with possession but has shown moments of danger, particularly in creating big chances, though they’ve failed to capitalize on key opportunities. Their defensive lapses early on allowed Genk to take the lead, but their late goal suggests they can exploit set pieces.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at the break
halftimeSections.text: For KRC Genk, Zakaria El Ouahdi has been the standout performer, scoring twice with composed finishes and proving to be a constant threat. Jarne Steuckers also deserves mention for his creative input, providing assists and pulling strings in midfield. On the Leuven side, Roggerio Nyakossi made a crucial impact with his headed goal, while players like Wouter George have contributed to chance creation despite limited possession. Both teams will look to these individuals to influence the game further in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum breakdown
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with Genk asserting dominance, showing consistent attacking intent and maintaining pressure for a significant period. Their momentum was evident through sustained spells of attack, particularly in the opening minutes and leading up to their two goals. Leuven, however, struggled to build momentum early on but grew into the game as the half progressed. Their attacking surges became more frequent towards the latter stages, culminating in their goal and a noticeable shift in energy just before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be a competitive affair as Leuven will likely push for an equalizer, buoyed by their late first-half goal. They’ll need to improve their finishing and capitalize on the big chances they create, while also tightening up defensively to prevent Genk from extending their lead. Genk, with their possession-based approach, will aim to manage the game and exploit any spaces left by Leuven’s forward push. Their attacking threat remains potent, and they could look to seal the game with another goal if Leuven overcommits.
